title: Install Podman on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS is very outdated
body:
### Issue Description

Following install instructions on Podmans website installs Feb 14th 2024 release.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ sudo apt update
$ sudo apt install podman
$ podman --version
podman version 4.9.3
```

### Describe the results you received

1.5 year old version

### Describe the results you expected

Latest version

### podman info output

```yaml
N/A
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Please report this to your distribution (or use a different one if it doesn't fit your use case), since they are responsible for packaging.  We have no control over what version is distributed there.

@giuseppe Hmm. The Podman team may not be able to update the official ubuntu package systems release, but you could create your own .deb packages for each github release, which can be installed much quicker than downloading and compiling source code and all its dependencies.

> Hmm. The Podman team may not be able
> to update the official ubuntu package systems release, but you could create
> your own .deb packages for each github release.

@KieranP Podman is a pretty complex software with a bunch of dependencies from the
containers universe for user space networking etc. I compiled Podman 5 on 24.04 in the 
past and newer Versions will require a lot more patching to work properly today. Don't 
expect them to build packages for Ubuntu. 

Ubuntu 24.04 is based on Debian Trixie and Sid. Trixie was released a month ago and 
ships Podman 5.4.2, and 5.4.1 is in Plucky. You can talk to the Ubuntu developers about 
putting podman into backports. I doubt this will happen tho.

Options are:
* You can use non-LTS Ubuntus till 26.04
* Migrate to Debian Trixie and help getting newer Podman versions into debian backports. 
  The Debian podman maintainer uploaded 5.6.1 to experimental some days ago. 
  People expressed their wish on the debian go mailinglist to have a current podman version 
  in backports. That needs more maintainers. Debian backports may end up in Ubuntu backports
  like the cockpit-podman packages. Otherwise you'll be stuck with Debian's or the next Ubuntu 
  LTS's version for the next 5 years.

ps: Another option I tested briefly today, but have not tested thoroughly, is installing Podman 
packages from Trixie or Plucky. They pull in a lot of depending packages. Some of them are 
essential for the base system, so I can't really recommend it.  Anyway, Plunky podman seems 
to work, Trixie doesn't at least for quadlets. My notes: 
https://gist.github.com/csenger/150e5fcd1e9ec2e756d0a4930e3d49ff.
